## [00:00 - 02:58]

So in the next two lessons I'll be talking about flagship onboarding products and complimentary products and the difference between them. So firstly we're going to look at flagship slash onboarding products. Now flagship and onboarding products are pivotal to the business because they're the products that bring you in brand new customers. You're going to go out to the market and you're going to say, there's this market I'm already selling to but I'm going to have a new onboarding flagship product and I'm going to go get a whole new market with that new product and when you get that right you can literally double your market overnight. But in doing that it is difficult because everything you may have done before to the existing market with the existing product may not necessarily translate or may not even help you do this second flagship or onboarding product. So you've got to understand that right from the word go. It may be expensive, there may be learning time that you need to learn what's important to these new customers and how to sell this new product. So we're quite locked our first product was an onboarding flagship product of the iPhone Bike Mount and we built the business around this product and had great success. And then we thought, okay we're going to take this approach that we had of a case with a mount and apply it to other verticals, other markets. So we took that same approach and applied it to running. Now applying it to running the product was similar but the market is completely different, the messaging is completely different, lots of it was completely different and to start with we actually had more, even though it was an onboarding flagship product, we actually had more success selling it as a complimentary product because we had to learn what is it that is important to runners, what's their problems they're having with the existing products on the market and how do we position that to them and how do we get them to see the value of the QuadLock Mech for a runner on their arm as they go about their daily runs. So take away from this flagship product section is probably that you're going to be doing a new flagship onboarding product to get you those new customers in that new market and a whole heap of new fresh sales in the door. And what you want to do is you want to leverage the business model but not necessarily the communication. So what you want to leverage is how you take orders, how you get them picked and packed, how you ship, all the upside of the business but the actual comms and the communication, the copy that you've been using in the existing market may not transfer to the new market. So yep, you can leverage what you've already built on the upside but communication, sales, marketing side, that may need to differ. So if you decided you want to do a new flagship onboarding product, use the workbook below to map out your strategy and we'll see you in the next lesson.
